message:
fix(printf): '*' width/precision now reads c_int and sign-extends (bd-0u5fm)

The extract_va_args! macro previously pulled 64 bits for each '*'
width/precision argument. By C11 7.21.6.1 para 5 these are `int`,
and on x86_64 System V varargs the upper 32 bits of a promoted c_int
register slot are undefined. Reading u64 directly gave garbage upper
bits, so render_printf's `raw_width as i64` check never saw a
negative value — the intended negative-width-implies-left-justify
(and negative-precision-ignored) paths were unreachable.

Switching to `$args.arg::<c_int>() as i64 as u64` preserves sign via
sign-extension, which the existing logic at lines ~3139-3163 of
render_printf already handles correctly. Removes
sprintf_star_neg_width and sprintf_star_neg_precision from
KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0qa3","title":"syscall: typed sysinfo veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:58:03.363438377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:05:43.092219780Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:05:43.089733386Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-0u5fm","title":"printf '*' negative width/precision: neg width should left-justify, neg precision should be ignored","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs, when width comes from '*' argument (Width::FromArg), a negative integer argument is not translated into '-' flag + abs(width) as C11 7.21.6.1 para 5 requires. Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance matrix (sprintf_star_neg_width case: format='%*d', args=[-5, 42] — expected '42   ', got 2000+ spaces). resolve_width() also returns 0 for FromArg which means it never reads the * argument at all; the two bugs compound.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:21:55.984065866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:36:55.804976827Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance matrix (sprintf_star_neg_width case: format='%*d', args=[-5, 42] — expected '42   ', got 2000+ spaces). resolve_width() also returns 0 for FromArg which means it never reads the * argument at all; the two bugs compound.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:21:55.984065866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:49:37.046999058Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0udd","title":"Metamorphic proptests for SigSet bitset algebra","description":"frankenlibc_core::signal::SigSet is a 64-bit signal bitmask with add/del/is_member/empty/full operations. Unit tests cover point cases but no algebraic MRs fence the bitset semantics. Add: (1) add-then-is_member, (2) del-reverses-add, (3) idempotent add, (4) invalid-sig rejection preserves state, (5) commutative add ordering, (6) full().is_member iff valid_signal. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/signal/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:24:38.440961830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.520499037Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.518577480Z","close_reason":"Added 7 SigSet MR proptests in commit 38830fa covering algebraic properties: invertive add/del, idempotent add, commutative order, invalid-sig state preservation, full()⇔valid_signal consistency, bulk add+del roundtrip, absorbing del-absent. ~1790 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-10pq","title":"Cross-cutting: Architecture heterogeneity (aarch64/x86_64/RISC-V)","description":"Multi-ISA support: 31 ISA targets with different atomic/futex semantics. Priority: x86_64 (primary), aarch64 (active bring-up), RISC-V (future). Must handle: different atomic ordering guarantees, futex syscall variations, SIMD multiarch dispatch (IFUNC-equivalent). SOS barrier certificates must be ISA-independent.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.526480149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:24:33.147137017Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","critique","cross-platform","feature-parity","frankenlibc","gap-closure","pthread","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-11g","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Pattern extraction from FrankenSQLite spec (systematic+repair+proof+tuning)","description":"Extract and restate the RaptorQ design pattern from FrankenSQLite in a runtime_math-compatible form.

Key spec concepts to port (pattern-level):
- Systematic fast path: read/store source symbols without GF(256) solve.
- Deterministic repair symbol generation from (ObjectId/epoch seed, K, symbol_size).
- Two overheads: additive decode slack + multiplicative loss budget.
- Optional adaptive overhead tuning using anytime-valid evidence (e-process) + evidence ledger.
- Explainable decode proofs on failure.

Deliverable:
- A short, self-contained design note (in bead comment) that future work can implement without re-reading the FrankenSQLite spec.","design":"Design note captured in-repo: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/raptorq_pattern_frankensqlite.md (systematic+repair symbols, deterministic repair schedule, overhead formula, anytime-valid tuning hook, decode proofs; strict/hardened constraints).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.102392443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006607654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006484483Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":624,"issue_id":"bd-11g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Note: RaptorQ Pattern (FrankenSQLite) Restated for glibc_rust Runtime

### Why We Want This In libc
We already have runtime_math controllers that make membrane decisions (Allow / FullValidate / Repair / Deny). When something bad happens we want an *audit-grade* trail that survives partial loss (ring-buffer overwrites, sampling, crashes, dropped events) and remains explainable.

RaptorQ's key “alien artifact” idea is **appendable redundancy**:
- emit cheap *systematic* symbols first (fast path)
- optionally emit deterministic *repair* symbols later (background cadence)
- decode and generate proofs offline (harness/tooling), not on the hot path

### Constraints (glibc_rust-specific)
- No `/dp/asupersync` runtime dependency. Runtime may implement a tiny encoder itself.
- Strict mode must not pay codec costs on the hot path.
- Hardened mode may pay bounded overhead but must remain deterministic and measurable.

### Portable Pattern (What We Copy)
1. **Source block / epoching**
   - Group a bounded window of evidence events into an *epoch*.
   - Epoch defines: `K_source` (number of systematic records), `T` (record/symbol size), and seed.
2. **Systematic fast path**
   - Emit/store raw evidence records as systematic symbols `ESI = 0..K_source-1`.
   - Layout should favor sequential capture/export (contiguous run) to minimize read overhead in tooling.
3. **Deterministic repair generation**
   - Generate repair symbols `ESI = K_source..K_source+R-1` deterministically from `(epoch_id, K_source, T, policy)`.
   - Determinism is the superpower: tooling can reproduce encoding schedule and validate.
4. **Overhead = additive slack + multiplicative budget**
   - Keep an additive decode slack `slack_decode` (FrankenSQLite V1 uses `+2`).
   - Add multiplicative overhead `overhead_percent` as needed.
5. **Anytime-valid tuning**
   - Use an anytime-valid monitor (e-process) to decide when to raise redundancy.
   - Record changes in an evidence ledger.
6. **Decode proofs**
   - When tooling decodes/repairs, it emits a proof artifact describing which symbols were used and why the decode is trusted.

### Concrete Formulas (From FrankenSQLite Spec Excerpts)
These are the “load-bearing” knobs we can reuse verbatim:

- Additive slack:
  - `slack_decode = 2` (default)

- Repair symbol count selection:
  - `R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))`

- Back-of-envelope loss fraction tolerated (engineering approximation):
  - `loss_fraction_max ≈ max(0, (R - slack_decode) / (K_source + R))`

### Deterministic Seed Derivation (Portable Rule)
FrankenSQLite uses a deterministic seed derived from a stable identifier (e.g., changeset id):
- `seed = xxh3_64(id_bytes)`

glibc_rust mapping recommendation:
- Define an `epoch_id` that is stable within-process (and includes a monotonic counter).
- Derive seed from `(epoch_id || build_id || mode)` to avoid collisions across runs.

### Record/Envelope Mapping (What We Emit)
FrankenSQLite’s SymbolRecord idea maps cleanly to libc evidence:

- **EvidenceSymbolRecord** (proposal)
  - Header: `{ magic, version, epoch_id, seqno, family, mode, action, flags }`
  - Coding: `{ esi, k_source, r_repair, symbol_size_T, seed }`
  - Integrity: `{ payload_hash (xxh3_128 or blake3), prev_hash (hash-chain) }`
  - Payload: fixed-size `T` bytes (small; e.g., 64–256B), or padded.

Notes:
- “Auth tags” are optional: for libc evidence we may just do hash-chains + optional MAC in tooling.
- Source symbols should be cheap to write (memcpy into ring buffer + atomic seqno).
- Repair symbols should be generated on cadence (every N decisions, timer tick, or on exit).

### Where Decode Happens
- Runtime: encode only (systematic always; repair optional).
- Harness/tooling: decode, verify hashes/chain, emit DecodeProof.

### Open Design Choice (Explicit)
We must choose whether runtime implements:
- a small XOR-only fountain (LT-like) code (fastest, simplest), or
- a tighter RaptorQ-compatible schedule (harder), or
- “RaptorQ-inspired” parity that tooling decodes with a matching decoder.

This is decided in `bd-3a9`.

### Acceptance Criteria For The Pattern Extraction
- A concrete mapping of the above into glibc_rust terms (epoch, record format, seed, R selection).
- Explicit non-goals: no GF(256) Gaussian solve on hot path; no asupersync runtime dep.
- A minimal test matrix for future implementation:
  - drop/bitflip simulation
  - deterministic reproduction
  - decode-proof verification","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:50:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vfl","title":"Docs: align README/FEATURE_PARITY with support taxonomy (no overclaim)","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + bottom-line.

Deliverables:
- Update README to include an explicit truth table:
  - what this is today (interposition vs replacement)
  - which subsystems are still call-through
  - which are stubs
- Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to reflect the same classification.

Acceptance:
- No claim of full replacement unless supported by the matrix + E2E harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.293544458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767467115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767444111Z","close_reason":"Aligned README/FEATURE_PARITY to support taxonomy snapshot; removed full-replacement overclaims; added hybrid interposition truth table","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs"]}
 {"id":"bd-vfze","title":"sysvipc: shmdt(NULL) should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for shmdt(NULL). Tighten the ABI regression test from a broad errno family to an exact host-parity assertion so the detachment null-pointer contract cannot drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:25:54.992564574Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:28:00.696755061Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:28:00.694339928Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity shmdt(NULL) behavior: exact EINVAL instead of a broad SysV IPC errno family; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-vgrav","title":"printf_conformance executor misrepresents u64 > i64::MAX as Double","description":"parse_printf_args() treats JSON integers larger than i64::MAX as PrintfArg::Double because as_i64 fails and the Double branch catches 'abs > i64::MAX as f64'. For %llu with ULLONG_MAX the dispatch then goes to the [Double] arm and varargs type mismatch produces wrong output. Needs a PrintfArg::Ulong (or equivalent unsigned path) and an explicit u64 branch in parse_printf_args. Surfaced by sprintf_llu_max in bd-12hh matrix.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:33:34.390958382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:46:29.385685109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-vgrav","title":"printf_conformance executor misrepresents u64 > i64::MAX as Double","description":"parse_printf_args() treats JSON integers larger than i64::MAX as PrintfArg::Double because as_i64 fails and the Double branch catches 'abs > i64::MAX as f64'. For %llu with ULLONG_MAX the dispatch then goes to the [Double] arm and varargs type mismatch produces wrong output. Needs a PrintfArg::Ulong (or equivalent unsigned path) and an explicit u64 branch in parse_printf_args. Surfaced by sprintf_llu_max in bd-12hh matrix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:33:34.390958382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:49:17.476098045Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:49:17.473899374Z","close_reason":"Added PrintfArg::Ulong(c_ulonglong) variant. parse_printf_args now detects u64 > i64::MAX and routes to Ulong before the f64 fallback check. Dispatch arms added to all four run_*_snprintf/fprintf functions. sprintf_llu_max (ULLONG_MAX) now passes end-to-end. Committed 0c1a8b3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vitcn","title":"syscall: signalfd4 valid mask with CLOEXEC plus invalid bit should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for signalfd(-1, &mask, SFD_CLOEXEC|8). Add focused ABI regression coverage for mixed valid+invalid flag bits.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:02.549780795Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:50.493732196Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:50.491621292Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(CLOEXEC|0x8) → EINVAL (unknown-bit rejection) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vjx5","title":"syscall: sched_setscheduler current pid with null param should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_setscheduler(0, SCHED_OTHER, NULL). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-param contract is explicit for the current process path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:21:58.922111839Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:48:03.490682959Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:48:03.488226124Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
